english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Philemon

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus vinctus Christi Jesu, et Timotheus frater, Philemoni dilecto, et adjutori nostro,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος δέσμιος χριστοῦ ἰησοῦ, καὶ τιμόθεος ὁ ἀδελφὸς, φιλήμονι τῷ ἀγαπητῷ καὶ συνεργῷ ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy *our* brother, unto Philemon our dearly beloved, and fellowlabourer,

verses.latin_vulgate: et Appiæ sorori carissimæ, et Archippo commilitoni nostro, et ecclesiæ, quæ in domo tua est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπφίᾳ τῇ ἀγαπητῇ, καὶ ἀρχίππῳ τῷ συστρατιώτῃ ἡμῶν, καὶ τῇ κατ᾽ οἶκόν σου ἐκκλησίᾳ
verses.king_james: And to *our* beloved Apphia, and Archippus our fellowsoldier, and to the church in thy house:

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia vobis, et pax a Deo Patre nostro, et Domino Jesu Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χάρις ὑμῖν καὶ εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν, καὶ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratias ago Deo meo, semper memoriam tui faciens in orationibus meis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐχαριστῶ τῷ θεῷ μου, πάντοτε μνείαν σου ποιούμενος ἐπὶ τῶν προσευχῶν μου
verses.king_james: I thank my God, making mention of thee always in my prayers,

verses.latin_vulgate: audiens caritatem tuam, et fidem, quam habes in Domino Jesu, et in omnes sanctos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούων σου τὴν ἀγάπην καὶ τὴν πίστιν ἣν ἔχεις πρὸς τὸν κύριον ἰησοῦν καὶ εἰς πάντας τοὺς ἁγίους
verses.king_james: Hearing of thy love and faith, which thou hast toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all saints;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut communicatio fidei tuæ evidens fiat in agnitione omnis operis boni, quod est in vobis in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅπως ἡ κοινωνία τῆς πίστεώς σου ἐνεργὴς γένηται ἐν ἐπιγνώσει παντὸς ἀγαθοῦ τοῦ ἐν ὑμῖν εἰς χριστὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: That the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gaudium enim magnum habui, et consolationem in caritate tua: quia viscera sanctorum requieverunt per te, frater.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χάριν γὰρ ἔχομεν πολλὴν καὶ παράκλησιν ἐπὶ τῇ ἀγάπῃ σου, ὅτι τὰ σπλάγχνα τῶν ἁγίων ἀναπέπαυται διὰ σοῦ, ἀδελφέ
verses.king_james: For we have great joy and consolation in thy love, because the bowels of the saints are refreshed by thee, brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod multam fiduciam habens in Christo Jesu imperandi tibi quod ad rem pertinet:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ πολλὴν ἐν χριστῷ παῤῥησίαν ἔχων ἐπιτάσσειν σοι τὸ ἀνῆκον
verses.king_james: Wherefore, though I might be much bold in Christ to enjoin thee that which is convenient,

verses.latin_vulgate: propter caritatem magis obsecro, cum sis talis, ut Paulus senex, nunc autem et vinctus Jesu Christi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τὴν ἀγάπην μᾶλλον παρακαλῶ τοιοῦτος ὢν ὡς παῦλος πρεσβύτης νυνὶ δὲ καὶ δέσμιος ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Yet for love's sake I rather beseech *thee*, being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: obsecro te pro meo filio, quem genui in vinculis, Onesimo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ σε περὶ τοῦ ἐμοῦ τέκνου ὃν ἐγέννησα ἐν τοῖς δεσμοῖς μου ὀνήσιμον
verses.king_james: I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my bonds:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui tibi aliquando inutilis fuit, nunc autem et mihi et tibi utilis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν ποτέ σοι ἄχρηστον νυνὶ δέ σοι καὶ ἐμοὶ εὔχρηστον
verses.king_james: Which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem remisi tibi. Tu autem illum, ut mea viscera, suscipe:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν ἀνέπεμψα σὺ δὲ αὐτὸν τουτέστι τὰ ἐμὰ σπλάγχνα προσλαβοῦ
verses.king_james: Whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him, that is, mine own bowels:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem ego volueram mecum detinere, ut pro te mihi ministraret in vinculis Evangelii:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν ἐγὼ ἐβουλόμην πρὸς ἐμαυτὸν κατέχειν ἵνα ὑπὲρ σοῦ διακονῇ μοι ἐν τοῖς δεσμοῖς τοῦ εὐαγγελίου
verses.king_james: Whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel:

verses.latin_vulgate: sine consilio autem tuo nihil volui facere, uti ne velut ex necessitate bonum tuum esset, sed voluntarium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χωρὶς δὲ τῆς σῆς γνώμης οὐδὲν ἠθέλησα ποιῆσαι ἵνα μὴ ὡς κατὰ ἀνάγκην τὸ ἀγαθόν σου ᾖ ἀλλὰ κατὰ ἑκούσιον
verses.king_james: But without thy mind would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity, but willingly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Forsitan enim ideo discessit ad horam a te, ut æternum illum reciperes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τάχα γὰρ διὰ τοῦτο ἐχωρίσθη πρὸς ὥραν ἵνα αἰώνιον αὐτὸν ἀπέχῃς
verses.king_james: For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever;

verses.latin_vulgate: jam non ut servum, sed pro servo carissimum fratrem, maxime mihi: quanto autem magis tibi et in carne, et in Domino?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκέτι ὡς δοῦλον ἀλλ᾽ ὑπὲρ δοῦλον ἀδελφὸν ἀγαπητὸν μάλιστα ἐμοὶ πόσῳ δὲ μᾶλλόν σοι καὶ ἐν σαρκὶ καὶ ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Not now as a servant, but above a servant, a brother beloved, specially to me, but how much more unto thee, both in the flesh, and in the Lord?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo habes me socium, suscipe illum sicut me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ οὖν ἐμὲ ἔχεις κοινωνὸν προσλαβοῦ αὐτὸν ὡς ἐμέ
verses.king_james: If thou count me therefore a partner, receive him as myself.

verses.latin_vulgate: si autem aliquid nocuit tibi, aut debet, hoc mihi imputa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τι ἠδίκησέν σε ἢ ὀφείλει τοῦτο ἐμοὶ ἐλλόγει
verses.king_james: If he hath wronged thee, or oweth *thee* ought, put that on mine account;

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego Paulus scripsi mea manu: ego reddam, ut non dicam tibi, quod et teipsum mihi debes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ παῦλος ἔγραψα τῇ ἐμῇ χειρὶ ἐγὼ ἀποτίσω ἵνα μὴ λέγω σοι ὅτι καὶ σεαυτόν μοι προσοφείλεις
verses.king_james: I Paul have written *it* with mine own hand, I will repay *it* : albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides.

verses.latin_vulgate: ita, frater. Ego te fruar in Domino: refice viscera mea in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ναὶ ἀδελφέ ἐγώ σου ὀναίμην ἐν κυρίῳ ἀνάπαυσόν μου τὰ σπλάγχνα ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Yea, brother, let me have joy of thee in the Lord: refresh my bowels in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confidens in obedientia tua scripsi tibi: sciens quoniam et super id, quod dico, facies.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πεποιθὼς τῇ ὑπακοῇ σου ἔγραψά σοι εἰδὼς ὅτι καὶ ὑπὲρ ὃ λέγω ποιήσεις
verses.king_james: Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say.

verses.latin_vulgate: Simul autem et para mihi hospitium: nam spero per orationes vestras donari me vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἅμα δὲ καὶ ἑτοίμαζέ μοι ξενίαν ἐλπίζω γὰρ ὅτι διὰ τῶν προσευχῶν ὑμῶν χαρισθήσομαι ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your prayers I shall be given unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutat te Epaphras concaptivus meus in Christo Jesu,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζονταί σε ἐπαφρᾶς ὁ συναιχμάλωτός μου ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: There salute thee Epaphras, my fellowprisoner in Christ Jesus;

verses.latin_vulgate: Marcus, Aristarchus, Demas, et Lucas, adjutores mei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μάρκος ἀρίσταρχος δημᾶς λουκᾶς οἱ συνεργοί μου
verses.king_james: Marcus, Aristarchus, Demas, Lucas, my fellowlabourers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia Domini nostri Jesu Christi cum spiritu vestro. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: Ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ μετὰ τοῦ πνεύματος ὑμῶν ἀμήν
verses.king_james: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ *be* with your spirit. Amen.
